msgs.text:
According to a document from 2026-06-28, Turn 129 is another attachment-only continuation. I used only the three files attached to this message: mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md.

Turn 129 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed The target remains the same over-G problem: whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS⟶∼ΓH such thatπH∘Φ=πS.This is exactly the condition stated in the attached prompt and problem statement.

The current two-coordinate state remains unchanged:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The attached history records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement:∃Φ:F⟶∼

F over GwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,henceΓS≅GΓH.This is exactly the requested over-G condition.No structural subcase should be reopened under the present inputs. The frozen proof chain remains:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+ Certificates D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The attached history gives the uniform reason: non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled, the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes by the Cm=Lm induction, the Ef-coefficient tower closes after exact normalization to (2,t) and the Koszul--Witt cascade, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite solutions.The compactness mechanism also remains the corrected filtered-Nielsen one: for coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms; each finite datum has invertible first Magnus layer and gives a finite over-G automorphism; compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit automorphism Φ:F→F, and intersecting all PN,J gives Φ(rS−1)=rH.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains unchanged. The current attachments contain only the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger; they do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1–D.4 artifacts such as independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED.

Thus the attachment-only transition rule remains idempotent:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 129 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 128. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN129-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
